sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
DOWN didn't work again - squares stayed at rows 14-17, cols 18-21 and 42-45. So DOWN is blocked from this position.
Let me think about what's happening. The squares can move LEFT/RIGHT but not DOWN. Let me check if UP works.
Also, I notice the squares are currently at cols 18-21 (left) and 42-45 (right). Looking at the black corridor map:
- Rows 14-17: B at cols 6-17, 22-29, 34-41, 46-57
So the squares are NOT in black regions. They're in colored regions (M at cols 18-21, p at cols 42-45).
But the squares moved LEFT successfully from cols 22-25 to 18-21. So they can move through colored regions horizontally.
But DOWN doesn't work. Maybe the squares can only move horizontally, not vertically? Or maybe there's a different rule.
Let me try UP to see if vertical movement works at all.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the level 1 behavior. In level 1, the squares moved through corridors. The corridors were black, and the squares moved through them in all directions (UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT).
In level 2, the squares moved LEFT but not DOWN. Maybe the rule is that the squares can only move in directions where there's a clear black path for BOTH squares.
Let me check:
- LEFT: Both squares had clear paths (B at cols 10-17 and 46-53). So LEFT worked.
- DOWN: Below left square (cols 18-21) is M at rows 18-21. Below right square (cols 42-45) is p at rows 18-21. So neither square has a clear black path down. DOWN didn't work.
- RIGHT: To the right of left square (cols 18-21) is... cols 22-25. At rows 14-17, cols 22-25 are B (from the black corridor map). To the right of right square (cols 42-45) is... cols 46-49. At rows 14-17, cols 46-49 are B. So RIGHT should work.
- UP: Above both squares (rows 6-13) is B. So UP should work.
Let me try UP to confirm vertical movement is possible.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# DOWN doesn't work. Let me try UP to see if vertical movement is possible
predict(expect_change=True, note='UP - squares should move up through black corridor')
action(['UP'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Blue: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Blue: rows 10-13, cols 18-21
Blue: rows 10-13, cols 42-45
